subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the poem Harvest Hymn by Sarojini Naidu
Recognize the importance of harvest and nature in agriculture
Identify natural forces like the sun, rain, and earth in the poem
Understand how farmers express gratitude to nature and the divine
Recognize poetic devices and symbolism in the poem
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Harvest Festivals
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the term hymn
Sharing examples of festivals like Pongal, Baisakhi, and Onam
student_activities: Discuss harvest festivals celebrated in their region
Answer questions about when and why harvest festivals are celebrated

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the poet and poem
student_activities: Match archaic words like thou, thee, thy with their meanings
Read the introductory part of the poem

lesson_plan.title: Men’s Voices in the Poem
topics_covered: Importance of sunlight and rain for crops
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the role of natural forces in farming
student_activities: Identify lines praising the sun and rain
Explain how sunlight and rain help crops grow

lesson_plan.title: Women’s Voices and Earth
teaching_strategy: Reading and discussing women’s voices in the poem
student_activities: Explain why Earth is described as a mother

lesson_plan.title: All Voices and Theme of Gratitude
topics_covered: Message of gratitude and harmony with nature
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the message of the poem
student_activities: Explain the meaning of 'Seed and Scythe'
Write the central message of the poem

lesson_plan.title: Language and Revision Activities
topics_covered: Farming tools mentioned in the lesson
student_activities: Match farming tools with their uses
Identify synonyms of words like munificent, radiance, and succour

teacher_tip: Encourage students to read the poem aloud with proper rhythm and expression. Discuss how farmers depend on nature and why expressing gratitude for natural resources is important.
